# growthbook_feature (Resource)

A GrowthBook feature flag, including its value type, default value, and per-environment enablement and targeting rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "growthbook_feature" "new_checkout" {
  id            = "new-checkout"
  description   = "Roll out the redesigned checkout flow"
  value_type    = "boolean"
  default_value = "false"
  project       = growthbook_project.web.id
  tags          = ["checkout", "growth"]

  environments = {
    production = {
      enabled = true
      rules = [
        {
          type        = "force"
          description = "Force on for internal users"
          value       = "true"
          condition   = jsonencode({ email = { "$regex" = "@example\\.com$" } })
        },
        {
          type           = "rollout"
          value          = "true"
          coverage       = 0.25
          hash_attribute = "id"
        },
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `default_value` (String) Default value returned when the feature is enabled (encoded as a string).
- `id` (String) Feature key. May contain letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores. Changing this forces a new feature.
- `value_type` (String) Data type of the feature value: `boolean`, `string`, `number`, or `json`. GrowthBook does not allow changing a feature's value type, so changing this forces a new feature.

### Optional

- `archived` (Boolean) Whether the feature is archived.
- `description` (String) Description of the feature.
- `environments` (Attributes Map) Per-environment configuration, keyed by environment ID. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments))
- `owner` (String) Owner userId or email address.
- `project` (String) Associated project ID.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags associated with the feature.

### Read-Only

- `date_created` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC3339).
- `date_updated` (String) Last update timestamp (RFC3339).
- `prerequisites` (List of String) Feature IDs that must evaluate to `true` for this feature (read-only).

<a id="nestedatt--environments"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the feature is enabled in this environment.
- `rules` (Attributes List) Ordered list of targeting rules evaluated in this environment. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules`

Required:

- `type` (String) Rule type: `force`, `rollout`, `experiment`, or `experiment-ref`.

Optional:

- `bucket_version` (Number) Sticky bucket version (experiment rules).
- `condition` (String) MongoDB-style targeting condition (JSON string).
- `coverage` (Number) Fraction of traffic included (rollout/experiment rules).
- `description` (String) Human-readable rule description.
- `disable_sticky_bucketing` (Boolean) Disable sticky bucketing (experiment rules).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `experiment_id` (String) Linked experiment ID (experiment-ref rules).
- `fallback_attribute` (String) Fallback bucketing attribute for sticky bucketing (experiment rules).
- `hash_attribute` (String) Attribute used for bucketing (rollout/experiment rules).
- `hash_version` (Number) Hash algorithm version (1 or 2).
- `min_bucket_version` (Number) Minimum sticky bucket version (experiment rules).
- `namespace` (Attributes) Namespace partition for experiment rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--namespace))
- `prerequisites` (Attributes List) Per-rule feature prerequisites. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--prerequisites))
- `saved_group_targeting` (Attributes List) Saved group targeting clauses. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--saved_group_targeting))
- `schedule_rules` (Attributes List) Time-based on/off schedule for the rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--schedule_rules))
- `seed` (String) Hash seed for bucketing (rollout rules).
- `sparse` (Boolean) JSON features only: merge the rule value onto the default instead of replacing it.
- `tracking_key` (String) Experiment tracking key (experiment rules).
- `value` (String) Value to serve (force/rollout rules).
- `values` (Attributes List) Weighted variation values (experiment rules). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--values))
- `variations` (Attributes List) Variation-to-value mappings (experiment-ref rules). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environments--rules--variations))

<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--namespace"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.namespace`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether namespace targeting is enabled.
- `name` (String) Namespace name.
- `range_max` (Number) Upper bound of the namespace range (0-1).
- `range_min` (Number) Lower bound of the namespace range (0-1).


<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--prerequisites"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.prerequisites`

Required:

- `condition` (String) Condition the prerequisite feature must satisfy (JSON string).
- `id` (String) Prerequisite feature ID.


<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--saved_group_targeting"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.saved_group_targeting`

Required:

- `match_type` (String) Match type: `all`, `any`, or `none`.
- `saved_groups` (List of String) Saved group IDs.


<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--schedule_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.schedule_rules`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Target enabled state at the timestamp.

Optional:

- `timestamp` (String) ISO timestamp when the rule changes state. Null means immediately.


<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.values`

Required:

- `value` (String) Variation value.
- `weight` (Number) Traffic weight (0-1).

Optional:

- `name` (String) Optional variation name.


<a id="nestedatt--environments--rules--variations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environments.rules.variations`

Required:

- `value` (String) Feature value for the variation.
- `variation_id` (String) Experiment variation ID.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Features are imported by their feature key.
terraform import growthbook_feature.new_checkout new-checkout
```
